name: Rahima Foundation
mission: Today, the world celebrates vast technological advancements, yet every 9th person suffers from food scarcity (1). The rate of food insecurity in the United States is even higher, standing at 11.8% (2). In the Silicon Valley, specifically, that rate more than doubles to 24.8%—making every 4th person food insecure (3). A total of 720,000 people out of 2.9 million Bay Area residents live with food insecurity (3).
theoryOfChange: Rahima Foundation states it provides a safety net to low-income community members through monthly groceries, rent and utility assistance, hot meals, and educational support, with a mission of creating equitable, resilient and inclusive communities starting with access to nutritious food for all.
scoreSummary: RAHIMA INTERNATIONAL FOUNDATION shows good alignment and moderate impact, with zakat compliance (significant risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Rahima Foundation lists "Zakat" as one of its programs on its website. Additionally, the charity platform "Feeling Blessed" explicitly states that Rahima Foundation is "Zakat eligible". (Source: https://www.rahima.org)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Context dependent profile. Consider cause fit, risk profile, and evidence maturity before deciding.

keyConcerns.headline: Zakat-collecting organization holds 65 months of reserves
keyConcerns.detail: Zakat must be distributed promptly per Islamic jurisprudence. Large reserves raise concerns about timely distribution.

keyConcerns.headline: Claims 9 geographic areas but no foreign grants on Schedule F
keyConcerns.detail: Organization lists international operations but IRS filings show no foreign grant activity.

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.improvement_suggestion: Clarify zakat allocation policy and eligible use categories for donors.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 29/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, domestic poverty cause area

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery, while keeping a clear operating reserve floor.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Seek external evaluation or track outcomes over at least 3 years.
components.name: Evidence & Outcomes

judge_issues.message: The impact rationale mentions a cost per beneficiary of $1,616.14, but doesn't explicitly state that this is considered high for domestic poverty interventions, which is a key factor in the medium impact score.
The impact rationale doesn't mention the weak board governance (2 members) as a factor contributing to the medium impact score.

The overall amal_score_rationale states that the charity has 'exceptional financial health'. While the charity has strong financial metrics, the risk deductions related to excessive reserves and small board size temper this assessment. 'Strong' or 'good' financial health might be more accurate.
Website citation for 'claims_zakat_eligible' uses homepage URL; deep link preferred

risks.risk_summary: Key concern: Board too small: 2 members

risks.description: Zakat-collecting organization holds 65 months of reserves — zakat must be distributed promptly
Claims 9 geographic areas but no foreign grants on Schedule F

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a moderate overall score due to its exceptional financial health and Zakat eligibility, balanced against a medium impact score and domestic poverty alignment.

Limited third-party impact data beyond financial reporting

dimension_explanations.alignment: With a score of 29/50, the charity shows medium alignment for donors interested in domestic poverty relief [1]. Its Zakat-eligible status and established volunteer opportunities provide a solid track record for community engagement [3][4].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high financial accountability with a Charity Navigator score of 96.0/100 and a 95.3% program expense ratio [1]. While it provides direct services with a high data confidence level, the calculated cost per beneficiary is approximately $1,616.14, resulting in a medium impact score of 21/50 [2].

baseline_narrative.headline: Rahima International Foundation provides critical humanitarian relief through high-efficiency food distribution and educational support programs.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 95.3%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach beneficiaries
Exceptional fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity with 65.0 months of working capital
baseline_narrative.summary: Based in California, Rahima International Foundation focuses on alleviating hunger and financial insecurity through direct service programs including a soup kitchen and meat collection [1][2]. The organization is recognized as Zakat-eligible, allowing Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations through various giving methods [3].

rich_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a moderate overall score due to its exceptional financial health and Zakat eligibility, balanced against a medium impact score and domestic poverty alignment.

areas_for_improvement.context: The organization currently operates with a <cite id="15">board size of only 2 members</cite>, which is below the recommended threshold for robust institutional oversight.
With <cite id="10">over 60 months of operating reserves</cite>, there is a significant opportunity to expand program reach rather than accumulating further assets.

bbb_assessment.summary: Financial data is audited, but governance standards regarding board size are likely not met.

case_against.mitigation_notes: The organization's massive reserve pool mitigates immediate financial risk from declining revenue.
risk_factors: <cite id="15">Board size of 2</cite> limits oversight and succession planning
<cite id="17">3-year revenue CAGR of -5.2%</cite> indicates declining public support
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="15">governance concentration</cite> and financial stagnation.

confidence.notes: Strong financial data availability; governance data is clear but highlights risks.

alignment.explanation: Strong fit for Muslim donors as a <cite id="5">Zakat-eligible</cite> entity serving <cite id="6">fuqara and masakin</cite> with a 33-year track record.
alignment.improvement: Clarify the specific <cite id="3">zakat allocation policy</cite> and eligible use categories to provide greater transparency for religious donors.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust this charity due to its <cite id="4">96.0/100 from Charity Navigator rating</cite> and <cite id="11">Gold Seal of Transparency</cite>. However, the <cite id="15">small board size</cite> presents a governance risk.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the foundation should expand its board to at least 5 independent members to ensure diverse oversight.

impact.explanation: Impact is driven by a <cite id="7">100.0% program ratio</cite>, though the <cite id="16">$1,616.14 cost per beneficiary</cite> suggests a high-intensity, high-cost service model.
impact.improvement: Publish a <cite id="10">time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves</cite> into mission delivery to maximize immediate community impact.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who prioritize maximum financial efficiency and want their Zakat to support direct, local hunger relief in Northern California.
donor_motivations: Want 95.3% of their donation to reach programs
Value long-standing community institutions with deep local roots
Prefer supporting direct service models like soup kitchens
Seeking a Zakat-eligible outlet with high transparency ratings
giving_considerations: The charity has significant cash reserves and may not have an urgent funding gap
Governance is concentrated in a very small board
Impact is localized to specific California counties
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking high-growth organizations or those who require rigorous third-party impact evaluations (RCTs).

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects <cite id="18">reported outcome metrics</cite> but a lack of independent external evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: The foundation provides direct nutritional and financial support to stabilize vulnerable families and students, enabling long-term self-sufficiency.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that high-cost interventions ($1,616 per person) are effectively breaking the cycle of poverty.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: While revenue is declining, the organization has <cite id="13">$14 million in assets</cite> to sustain operations.

organizational_capacity.geographic_reach: San Mateo and Santa Clara Counties, CA

peer_comparison.differentiator: Distinguished by an <cite id="8">unbeatable fundraising efficiency</cite> and massive reserve levels compared to the <cite id="9">89.8% peer median program ratio</cite>.

similar_organizations.differentiator: Similar size and community service focus
similar_organizations.name: Texas Muslim Womens Foundation Inc

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="7">95.3% of its expenses toward programs</cite>, significantly exceeding the <cite id="9">peer program ratio median of 89.8%</cite>.
Rahima maintains <cite id="10">65.0 months of working capital</cite>, providing an extraordinary level of financial cushion for its operations.
The charity holds a <cite id="11">Gold Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and a <cite id="12">96/100 accountability score</cite> from Charity Navigator.

strengths_deep_dive: Rahima International Foundation demonstrates a remarkable commitment to fiscal responsibility, as evidenced by its <cite id="8">$0.00 fundraising cost per dollar raised</cite>. This efficiency ensures that donor contributions are utilized almost entirely for direct service delivery. Furthermore, the organization has built a massive asset base, reporting <cite id="13">$14,151,875 in total assets</cite> against negligible liabilities, which secures its long-term ability to serve the community. Its <cite id="14">95.3% independent board</cite> further reinforces its commitment to objective governance and oversight.
rich_narrative.summary: Founded in <cite id="1">1993</cite>, Rahima International Foundation is a California-based humanitarian organization dedicated to alleviating hunger and financial insecurity within local communities. The organization operates with a high degree of financial efficiency, reporting <cite id="2">$4,006,022 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 tax year. Rahima is distinguished by its direct service model, which includes <cite id="3">food distribution, a soup kitchen, and meat collection</cite> programs primarily serving San Mateo and Santa Clara counties. The charity maintains a <cite id="4">96/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting strong performance in accountability and transparency. As a <cite id="5">Zakat-eligible organization</cite>, it provides a critical avenue for Muslim donors to support the <cite id="6">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> through verified religious giving channels. Despite its long operational history of over 30 years, the foundation maintains a lean administrative profile, achieving a <cite id="7">95.3% program expense ratio</cite> and a <cite id="8">fundraising efficiency of $0.00 per $1 raised</cite>.
